findings.title: Bangladesh Central Bank Chief Quits Abruptly After Change in Government - Bloomberg
findings.description: The head of Bangladesh’s central bank stepped down in a surprise departure that comes just over a week after the inauguration of the country’s new democratically elected government.

findings.title: Monetary policy decisions - European Central Bank
findings.description: Combined monetary policy decisions and statementDownload PDFMonetary policy statement (with Q&A)View page · PRESS RELEASE · 5 February 2026 · The Governing Council today decided to keep the three key ECB interest rates unchanged.

findings.title: A year in which the major central banks part ways | Reuters
findings.description: Central banks in big economies are parting ways, with Australia this week raising interest rates for the first time in two years, while others are taking a more cautious approach even if they are likely done with easing.

findings.title: Fed signals rate hikes still possible in 2026 - TheStreet
findings.description: View post: Fed official signals surprise rate-cut shift ... Softer inflation data revive hopes for easing borrowing costs in 2026.

findings.title: Our monetary policy statement at a glance - February 2026
findings.description: Firms are asking for more credit, but banks are also looking more carefully at who they lend to. ... Here is what the Governing Council decided about the ECB`s interest rates and instruments at its latest meeting. Press release · Read our explanation of the reasons behind the latest monetary policy

findings.title: S.Korea central bank holds rates, adopts dot-plot path signalling extended pause | Reuters
findings.description: South Korea's central bank stood pat on interest rates on Thursday and signalled policy would stay unchanged for the next six months as a chip boom in exports and steady inflation allow policymakers more time to assess financial stability risks.

findings.title: Week Ahead Economic Preview: Week of 23 February 2026 | S&P Global
findings.description: Upcoming data will help guide markets on central bank policy, and in particular whether Japan and Australia might soon see further rate hikes.

findings.title: February Global Regulatory Brief: Digital finance | Insights | Bloomberg Professional Services
findings.description: As technology continues to reshape financial services, regulators and policy setters are embarking on a range of digital-finance initiatives to manage risks and set appropriate standards.
